title: 6 skills every engineer needs for the AI era
description: Beyond boosting efficiency, the strongest software developers use AI to explore the problem space and find more creative solutions. Figma’s Vice President of Software Engineering Marcel Weekes and Developer Advocate Jake Albaugh share the crucial skills engineers should learn to meet the moment.

title: Bill McDermott joins Figma’s Board of Directors
description: Bill McDermott, the Chairman and CEO of ServiceNow, is joining Figma’s Board of Directors.

title: Figma Files Registration Statement for Proposed Initial Public Offering
description: We’re sharing an update on Figma’s path to becoming a publicly traded company. After submitting a confidential draft of our S-1 to the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) in April, it is now available to the public.

title: Figma moves closer to its community with Latin American Spanish localization
description: Latin American Spanish becomes Figma’s latest language for product and support localization.

title: Rolling out Santa without freezing productivity: Tips from securing Figma’s fleet
description: We scaled Santa, an open-source binary authorization tool, across all Figmates’ laptops to boost endpoint security while keeping workflows seamless. Here’s how we tackled the challenges and ensured a smooth rollout.

title: How we shaped the visual identity for Config 2025
description: From giant inflatable glyphs to welcoming soundscapes, Figma’s Brand Studio designed an immersive conference that celebrated the spirit of makership at every turn.

title: In good company: How retailers use Figma to elevate e-commerce
description: Brands like Nuuly, Ruggable, and GitHub are designing multidimensional experiences that build customer trust, serve employee needs, and uphold core values.

title: Why development leaders are investing in design
description: An IDC study shows just how central design has become to development leaders.

title: Figma Announces Launch of Initial Public Offering Roadshow
description: Today, we’re announcing the launch of the roadshow for Figma’s proposed initial public offering of Class A common stock.

title: Mike Krieger and Luis von Ahn join Figma’s Board of Directors
description: Mike Krieger, CPO of Anthropic, and Luis von Anh, co-founder and CEO of Duolingo, are joining Figma’s Board of Directors.

title: Figma deepens roots in Australia with local data hosting and more
description: Figma introduces new governance features for enterprise customers, with local data residency expected later this year.

title: Prompt, prototype, perfect: Figma Make is now available to all users
description: Today, all Figma AI features and products are moving out of beta, including Figma Make—which is now available for everyone to try. Here’s how teams are using the prompt-to-app tool to dream bigger, move faster, and work better together.

title: The making of a product icon
description: Creating product icons at Figma involves dozens—sometimes hundreds—of iterations. Product Designer Tim Van Damme shares his thoughtful approach to icon design and the creative exploration that shapes each final result.

title: Figma Announces Increase in Initial Public Offering Price Range
description: Following the launch of Figma’s roadshow last week, we’re announcing an increased price range for our proposed IPO.

title: A tale of two parameter architectures—and how we unified them
description: After launching variables and component properties in quick succession, we were left with two different underlying architectures for parametrization. Here’s how we unified them for consistency and scalability.

title: Figma Announces Pricing of Initial Public Offering
description: Tomorrow, July 31, 2025, Figma plans to begin trading on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol “FIG.”

title: Figma's IPO: Design is everyone's business
description: As Figma goes public, our commitment endures—to eliminate the gap between imagination and reality. Read Dylan Field’s founder letter about why design is more important than ever, and what’s next for the company.

title: The Duolingo method: Collaboration as a core practice
description: Duolingo’s Math team ditches traditional handoff in favor of co-creation, scrappy prototypes, and constant experimentation.

title: Who says design needs a mouse?
description: Figma's new accessibility features bring better keyboard and screen reader support to all creators.

title: Design systems and AI: Why MCP servers are the unlock
description: Paired with MCP servers, design systems become a productivity coefficient for AI-powered workflows, ensuring that AI agents produce output that’s relevant and on brand.

title: Getting into the groove: How music shaped the scatter brushes in Figma Draw
description: Today, we’re adding 10 new scatter brushes to push the creative possibilities in Figma Draw. Here’s how the team drew inspiration from Doo-wop, Vaporwave, and other music genres to design them.

title: 8 tips for designers building branded templates in Figma Buzz
description: To create self-serve templates that bend—but don’t break—brand designers need to consider work through a marketing lens. Here’s how to set up editing guardrails and design layouts that flex and scale.

title: The anatomy of an activation: How Figma Commons brought design to the public
description: On July 31, a huge Figma banner covered the New York Stock Exchange building with a message that read “Design is everyone’s business.” As Figma stepped into its next chapter, we wanted to celebrate in a way that kept that idea front and center.

title: Forrester analyzes the ROI of Dev Mode
description: For developers, working more efficiently isn’t just about being faster, but also reducing friction in their workflows. Forrester tells us how to save them time and headache—all in service of shipping better products.

title: Version control: How Figma Make helped us figure out video playback
description: To get videos working just right in Figma Buzz, Product Designer Natasha Tenggoro used Figma Make to prototype complex ideas when words alone fell short. We show three of them here, prompts and all.

title: IDC study says the global workforce engaged in software design is expanding
description: IDC forecasts that the number of people involved in software design will increase by more than 30% from 2025 to 2029—signaling the rising importance of design as a competitive differentiator.

title: Why is corpcore suddenly such a thing?
description: From vintage Apple tees fetching hundreds on Grailed to Supreme-level lines at tech conferences, it seems like software swag has upped its swag factor. Here’s why wearing your app on your sleeve has gone from cringeworthy to cool.

title: How to build a resilient design team
description: In an environment that demands speed and agility, how do you build a team that’s resilient to change, no matter what’s around the corner?

title: How to harness skills that AI can’t automate
description: With more teams moving faster than ever, the real differentiator is craft—the curiosity, intuition, taste, and intention behind every detail.

title: The anatomy of a summer campaign: How Instacart staged a sick ’90s throwback
description: Fish-eye lens, oversize tees, and a free concert with Third Eye Blind—here’s how Instacart’s creative studio and marketing teams pulled off a nostalgic summer campaign featuring our favorite snacks from 1999.

title: Are roles and responsibilities a thing of the past?
description: For years, the boundaries between product development roles have become less defined. Our latest report quantifies this shift and explores what it means for you and your team.

title: Issue no.12: New roles, new rules
description: With faster iteration cycles and AI tools helping people stretch further up the stack, more product builders are reinventing their roles.

title: Is the app layer where AI proves its value?
description: The next leap in AI won’t come from new models, but from an app layer that makes the technology more intuitive and useful for everyone.

title: Free association: Production designer Jeremy Hindle on building Severance
description: From Jacque Tati’s “Playtime” to David Lynch’s “Twin Peaks,” Jeremy Hindle traces the ideas and images that shaped Lumon’s uncanny world.

title: Figma rendering: Powered by WebGPU
description: We’ve updated our renderer to use WebGPU, unlocking new performance optimization opportunities. Here’s how we did it.

title: The branding for Intercom’s AI summit looks like an alien superbloom
description: For the customer service and AI event Pioneer, Intercom’s designers used Figma to create a visual language of surreal flowers and shape-shifting pollen.

title: Design context, everywhere you build
description: Our latest updates to the Figma MCP server and Code Connect bring Figma design context anywhere you work.

title: Why you should care about design context
description: As developers increasingly use agentic coding tools, the structure and clarity of design files matters more than ever. By thoughtfully organizing your work, you can help your developer counterparts build more efficiently and accurately.

title: Bringing Figma Make to the canvas
description: Now you can copy any design from a Figma Make preview to the design canvas, allowing you to edit, iterate, and take your ideas further.

title: Turn your ChatGPT brainstorms into FigJam diagrams
description: With the new Figma app in ChatGPT, you can turn conversations into FigJam diagrams like flow charts, Gantt charts, and more.

title: 6 winning Figma Makes—and what you can learn from them
description: Winners from our first global Make-a-thon offer insights on how to prototype smarter, structure products better, and push Figma Make further.

title: 4 ways for design teams to chart new territory with Figma Make
description: From redrawing product roadmaps to building starter templates, these Figma Make ideas from Maven Clinic, Pendo, ServiceNow, and LinkedIn show how designers can prompt a path forward.

title: 15+ ways we’re improving accessibility in Figma
description: To make Figma easier to navigate for all, we’re launching over a dozen keyboard-only controls and improving the screen reader experience.

title: Design systems: From the basics to big things ahead
description: To prepare for Schema 2025, we combed through Shortcut archives to pull together our design systems greatest hits.

title: Visibility at scale: How Figma detects sensitive data exposure
description: Solving security challenges at scale requires creativity as much as rigor. To reduce the risk of sensitive data exposure, we built Response Sampling: a lightweight, real-time control that watches outbound responses, validates access, and provides an early warning system across our products.

title: Three new features to help build brand momentum with Figma Buzz
description: With marketing templates that are now connected to your design system, plugins that pull in your favorite apps, and video trimming in Figma Buzz, we’re giving designers and marketers new tools to create branded assets that speak volumes.

title: Schema 2025: Design systems for a new era
description: Design systems help teams push what’s possible while maintaining a high level of craft, polish, and performance. Here’s everything we announced at Schema by Figma to help teams design for the AI era.

title: Introducing Figma Weave: The next generation of AI-native creation at Figma
description: Figma has acquired Weavy, a platform that brings generative AI and professional editing tools into the open canvas. As Figma Weave, the company will help build out image, video, animation, motion design, and VFX media generation and editing capability on the Figma platform.

title: Issue no.13: All systems go
description: Figma’s editorial newsletter about how products take shape—and shape our world.

title: ServiceNow and Figma launch strategic collaboration to turn design vision into enterprise transformation
description: Today, ServiceNow, the AI platform for business transformation, and Figma announced a strategic collaboration to turn design vision into enterprise transformation.

title: Figma opens a new hub in India to support our growing community of builders
description: With our new hub in Bengaluru, we’re investing in India’s fast-growing product development community.

title: Gemini 3 Pro is now available in Figma Make
description: We ran Gemini 3 Pro through a series of design exercises in Figma Make. Here’s what stood out.

title: 5 shifts redefining design systems in the AI era
description: As AI reshapes how we make products, design systems are evolving from libraries of reusable parts into living frameworks that scale taste and craft. We spoke with product leaders and practitioners about the shifts they’re seeing in how design systems are built, used, and maintained.

title: Creativity meets precision with Google's Nano Banana Pro
description: From quick style variations to precise edits, Nano Banana Pro helps teams stay creative without losing context or brand integrity.

title: Version control: One founder’s mission to save local farms with Figma Make
description: Factory farms and rising costs are putting British Columbia’s small growers out of business at an alarming rate. To fight back, entrepreneur Aaron Veale used Figma Make to build a marketplace app that helps farmers sell their produce—all in a matter of weeks.

title: Double click: What does it mean to be a designer in the age of AI?
description: The scope of what designers, developers, and product managers do is expanding, and the boundaries between them are blurring. As workflows shift, a bigger question emerges: What happens to job titles—and the ways of working and sense of identity that comes with them?

title: Figma expands support for India with local data hosting and new governance tools
description: Figma introduces new governance features for enterprise customers, with local data residency expected next quarter.

title: Updates to AI credits in Figma
description: We’re introducing new ways to track your AI credit usage in Figma, and soon you’ll have more ways to buy additional AI credits.

title: Introducing three new tools for precise image editing in Figma
description: Today we’re introducing three new AI image editing tools that make precision editing faster, cleaner, and more intuitive—powering a more complete creative workflow in Figma.

title: Figma Make is now available in Figma for Government
description: With Figma Make now available in Figma for Government, teams can prototype faster, collaborate securely, and accelerate progress on modernizing public services.

title: Turn your prompts into visual assets and slide decks with the Figma app in ChatGPT
description: We’re expanding the capabilities of the Figma app in ChatGPT, so you can turn your ideas into creative assets and presentations that you can polish in Figma Buzz and Figma Slides.

title: Hard problems are still hard: A story about the tools that change and the work that doesn't
description: Figma designer–turned–product manager Nikolas Klein worked on building prototyping tools for seven years. Then AI changed the game.

title: Prototypes are the new PRDs
description: A growing number of product managers are finding that the fastest way to clarity is to build. Inside Figma Make, they’re pressure-testing assumptions early, building momentum, and rallying teams around something tangible.

title: Chat, are we cooked? How language has become the new metric of virality
description: We sat down with linguist Adam Aleksic, author of “Algospeak: How Social Media Is Transforming the Future of Language,” to learn how algorithms shape not just how we talk, but how we think and relate.

title: Cooking with constraints: A designer’s framework for better AI prompts
description: Design and cooking share a truth: Preparation determines the outcome. Structured prompts turn AI from guesswork into a reliable design partner.

title: Meet the maker: Kelsey Fairhurst’s irresistibly bold flatware
description: Brooklyn-based designer Kelsey Fairhurst launched Forks Plus, a “softline brutalist” flatware brand, with the help of Figma.

title: Press start: How controllers shaped video game design—and where interfaces may go next
description: Aashrey Sharma, a UX designer at Epic Games, shares what video game navigation can teach us about building the next generation of interfaces.

title: Software is culture
description: The software we scroll, tap, pinch, and swipe has irrevocably shaped the way we think and feel. As that software grows more intelligent, we look back on the most influential interactions from the past 20 years to see where we may be headed next.

title: Issue no.14: Software is culture
description: From ordering delivery in a pinch, to finding genuine connection and meaning, software touches nearly every part of our lives.

title: The new business case for design systems
description: Design systems have evolved from static libraries to key drivers of business revenue, customer loyalty, and product strategy. Here’s what you need to know about how to track and communicate the value of your design system, based on new research from the Design Executive Council (DXC).

title: Show don't tell: Embed Make prototypes everywhere you work in Figma
description: Today, we’re introducing the ability to embed Figma Make prototypes into Figma Design, FigJam, and Figma Slides, along with new editing tools that help you build and share your best ideas.

title: Think outside of the box—with Claude and FigJam
description: With the Figma MCP app in Claude, powered by Anthropic, designers, developers, and product managers can now create AI-generated FigJam diagrams.

title: Figma achieves C5 accreditation, strengthening cloud security for customers across the DACH region
description: Figma achieves C5 accreditation, strengthening cloud security for customers across the DACH region.

title: For the love of craft: Vectorize images in Figma
description: With our new AI image editing tool, Vectorize, you can turn raster images into editable vectors, allowing you to tweak, refine, and scale designs directly in Figma.

title: Why demand for designers is on the rise
description: In the AI era, companies need designers more than ever. In fact, our latest study suggests that AI is actually driving renewed momentum in design hiring. We unpack why that is, what hiring managers are prioritizing, and which skills designers need to get ahead.

title: State of the Designer 2026: Designers are leaning into the messy middle
description: Our State of the Designer report explores how designers around the world are upleveling their skills, keeping craft high, and turning new pressures into creative momentum.

title: From Claude Code to Figma: Turning production code into editable Figma designs
description: Now you can take workflows that start in Claude Code even further in Figma.

title: The future of design is code and canvas
description: There isn't just one way to build. For the best ideas to move forward, we need the power of code and the canvas. Claude Code to Figma is just one way we’re giving builders more choice.

title: Redefining impact as a data scientist
description: Not all impactful data science work involves experiments or optimization. Sometimes it’s about making complex systems legible, correct, and safe to operate.

title: Building frontend UIs with Codex and Figma
description: With Codex to Figma, teams can bring real, running interfaces into Figma to explore, refine, and make decisions together, then bring it back to Codex with design context intact.

title: Workflow lab: AI image tooling and interactive prototyping in Figma
description: A workflow to try—bringing together precise image editing, Vectorize, and interactive prototypes to move from concept to crit to completion faster.

title: Our Config 2026 speakers on the biggest opportunities with AI
description: Here’s a first look at our speakers and how they’re interrogating craft, quality, and intention in an AI-powered world.

title: 3 ways product teams are building conviction faster with Figma Make
description: From communicating complex behaviors to pushing the edges of an idea, product managers at ServiceNow, Ticketmaster, and Affirm are using Figma Make to prototype their way forward.

title: How to supercharge your design system with slots
description: Slots give you the ability to customize components without breaking the system. We’re sharing five field-tested tips from early users to help you unlock more freedom without giving up control.

title: Vishal Kapoor’s 10 rules for building honest products with AI
description: Affirm SVP of Product Vishal Kapoor shares 10 non-negotiable rules for harnessing AI to build products that are clear, secure, and fundamentally honest.

title: 5 design skills to sharpen in the AI era
description: Our latest research highlights the skills designers need to meet the moment.

title: Issue no.15: The state of design
description: Figma’s editorial newsletter about how products take shape—and shape our world.

title: Design’s influence is expanding, and here’s why that feels hard
description: As AI accelerates creation and raises expectations, designers are navigating a field that’s bigger, faster, and more demanding than ever.

title: How we rebuilt the foundations of component instances
description: By replacing a decade-old architecture with a reactive foundation, we made common operations in large design systems up to 50% faster and unlocked a new way to build dynamic features.

title: Agents, meet the Figma canvas
description: Starting today, you can use AI agents to design directly on the Figma canvas. And with skills, you can guide agents with context about your team’s decisions and intent.

title: Figma's next-generation data caching platform
description: Scalability and reliability gaps in our Redis platform were a growing threat to Figma's site availability. This is the story of how we built FigCache, a foundational technology that underpins a reimagined internal platform for ephemeral data.

title: 6 designs that reimagine how we interact with software
description: From a multiplayer embroidery sampler to a photo booth that works across time zones, this year's Make-a-thon winners offer new ways to connect, reflect, and play through software.

title: Build with more context and more control in Figma Make
description: Starting today, Make kits and Make attachments bring context into Figma Make, so prototypes start from real components, data, and constraints.

title: How Figma AI tools helped us bring extra delight to the canvas
description: For our annual April Fun Day, we used Figma Make, Weave, and MCP to build six mini games that captured the spirit of play. Here’s what we learned.

title: Turning prompts into five scalable workflows with Figma Weave
description: To build upon a visual language with AI, you need to harness the logic behind each prompt—building, editing, and directing how visuals come to life. Here, we share five creative workflows that show the breadth of what the Figma Weave canvas can do.

title: The TL;DR on MCP: Why context matters and how to put it to work
description: Figma’s MCP server brings your design decisions into the tools where code gets written—so what gets built actually matches what was designed. Here’s what that unlocks for everyone who builds products.

title: How AI leaders are borrowing from the design playbook
description: A new class of leaders is leaning on design principles to guide their companies through AI transformation.

title: FigJam is now your coding agent’s whiteboard too
description: Agents are changing your code faster than your team can follow. Now you can close that gap with new MCP skills, architecture layouts, and more in FigJam.

title: How to design agentic tools for work
description: The Gemini Enterprise team shares their approach to making complex, multi-agent workflows feel simple, intuitive, and trustworthy.

title: Workflow lab: Expanding the canvas with Figma MCP
description: As teams build faster, design and code can drift apart just as quickly. This workflow shows how to close that gap—bringing real product states onto the canvas so designers can shape what actually ships.

title: 6 skills every engineer needs for the AI era
description: Figma’s Vice President of Software Engineering Marcel Weekes and Developer Advocate Jake Albaugh share the crucial skills engineers should learn to meet the moment.

description: With Figma Make now available in Figma for Government, teams can prototype faster, collaborate securely, and accelerate progress on modernizing public services.

title: Turn your prompts into visual assets and slide decks with the Figma app in ChatGPT
description: We’re expanding the capabilities of the Figma app in ChatGPT, so you can turn your ideas into creative assets and presentations that you can polish in Figma Buzz and Figma Slides.

title: Hard problems are still hard: A story about the tools that change and the work that doesn't
description: Figma designer–turned–product manager Nikolas Klein worked on building prototyping tools for seven years. Then AI changed the game.

title: Prototypes are the new PRDs
description: Inside Figma Make, product managers are pressure-testing assumptions early, building momentum, and rallying teams around something tangible.

title: Chat, are we cooked? How language has become the new metric of virality
description: Linguist Adam Aleksic, author of “Algospeak: How Social Media Is Transforming the Future of Language,” explains how algorithms shape how we talk, think, and relate.

title: Cooking with constraints: A designer’s framework for better AI prompts
description: Design and cooking share a truth: Preparation determines the outcome. Structured prompts turn AI from guesswork into a reliable design partner.

title: Meet the maker: Kelsey Fairhurst’s irresistibly bold flatware
description: Brooklyn-based designer Kelsey Fairhurst launched Forks Plus, a “softline brutalist” flatware brand, with the help of Figma.

title: Press start: How controllers shaped video game design—and where interfaces may go next
description: Aashrey Sharma, a UX designer at Epic Games, shares what video game navigation can teach us about building the next generation of interfaces.

title: Software is culture
description: Yesterday's software has shaped today's generation. To understand what's next as software grows more intelligent, we look back on 20 years of interaction design.

title: Issue no.14: Software is culture
description: Figma’s editorial newsletter about how products take shape—and shape our world.

title: The new business case for design systems
description: What you need to know about how to track and communicate the value of your design system

title: Show don't tell: Embed Make prototypes everywhere you work in Figma
description: Today, we’re introducing the ability to embed Figma Make prototypes into Figma Design, FigJam, and Figma Slides, along with new editing tools.

title: Think outside of the box—with Claude and FigJam
description: With the Figma MCP app in Claude, powered by Anthropic, designers, developers, and product managers can now create AI-generated FigJam diagrams.

title: Figma achieves C5 accreditation, strengthening cloud security for customers across the DACH region
description: Figma achieves C5 accreditation, strengthening cloud security for customers across the DACH region.

title: For the love of craft: Vectorize images in Figma
description: With our new AI image editing tool, Vectorize, you can turn raster images into editable vectors, allowing you to tweak, refine, and scale designs directly in Figma.

title: Why demand for designers is on the rise
description: Our latest study suggests that AI is driving renewed momentum in design hiring. We unpack why that is, what hiring managers prioritize, and which skills designers need to get ahead.

title: State of the Designer 2026: Designers are leaning into the messy middle
description: Our State of the Designer report explores how designers are balancing uncertainty with optimism and using AI to uplevel their craft.

title: From Claude Code to Figma: Turning production code into editable Figma designs
description: Now you can take workflows that start in Claude Code even further in Figma.

title: The future of design is code and canvas
description: For the best ideas to move forward, we need the power of code and the canvas. Claude Code to Figma is just one way we’re giving builders more choice.

title: Redefining impact as a data scientist
description: Not all impactful data science work involves experiments or optimization. Sometimes it’s about making complex systems legible, correct, and safe to operate.

title: Building frontend UIs with Codex and Figma
description: With Codex to Figma, teams can bring real, running interfaces into Figma to explore, refine, and make decisions together, then bring it back to Codex with design context intact.

title: Workflow lab: AI image tooling and interactive prototyping in Figma
description: A workflow to try—bringing together precise image editing, Vectorize, and interactive prototypes to move from concept to crit to completion faster.

title: Our Config 2026 speakers on the biggest opportunities with AI
description: Here’s a first look at our speakers and how they’re interrogating craft, quality, and intention in an AI-powered world.

title: 3 ways product teams are building conviction faster with Figma Make
description: Product managers at ServiceNow, Ticketmaster, and Affirm are using Figma Make to prototype their way forward.

title: How to supercharge your design system with slots
description: Slots give you the ability to customize components without breaking the system. We’re sharing five field-tested tips from early users to help you unlock more freedom without giving up control.

title: Vishal Kapoor’s 10 rules for building honest products with AI
description: Affirm SVP of Product Vishal Kapoor shares 10 non-negotiable rules for harnessing AI to build products that are clear, secure, and fundamentally honest.

title: 5 design skills to sharpen in the AI era
description: Our latest research highlights the skills designers need to meet the moment.

description: Figma’s editorial newsletter about how products take shape—and shape our world.

title: Design’s influence is expanding, and here’s why that feels hard
description: As AI accelerates creation and raises expectations, designers are navigating a field that’s bigger, faster, and more demanding than ever.

title: How we rebuilt the foundations of component instances
description: By replacing a decade-old architecture with a reactive foundation, we made common operations in large design systems up to 50% faster and unlocked a new way to build dynamic features.

title: Agents, meet the Figma canvas
description: Starting today, you can use AI agents to design directly on the Figma canvas. And with skills, you can guide agents with context about your team’s decisions and intent.

title: Figma's next-generation data caching platform
description: This is the story of how we built FigCache, a foundational technology that underpins a reimagined internal platform for ephemeral data.

title: Build with more context and more control in Figma Make
description: Starting today, Make kits and Make attachments bring context into Figma Make, so prototypes start from real components, data, and constraints.

title: Turning prompts into five scalable workflows with Figma Weave

description: Here’s what MCP unlocks for everyone who builds products.

description: A new class of leaders is leaning on design principles to guide their companies through AI transformation.

description: Agents are changing your code faster than your team can follow. Now you can close that gap with new MCP skills, architecture layouts, and more in FigJam.

description: Here’s how the Gemini Enterprise team makes complex, multi-agent workflows feel simple, intuitive, and trustworthy.

title: Workflow lab: Expanding the canvas with Figma MCP
